title: विन्ध्यवासिनी स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री विन्ध्यवासिनी स्तोत्रम् ॥

ॐ नमो भगवत्यै विन्ध्यवासिन्यै॥

विन्ध्याचलनिवासिन्यै नारायण्यै नमो नमः।
सर्वेश्वर्यै महादेव्यै चण्डिकायै नमो नमः॥१॥

विन्ध्ये विन्ध्यनिवासिनि प्रणमतां सौभाग्यसम्पत्प्रदे
दुर्गे दुर्गतिनाशिनि प्रणमतां सर्वार्थसम्पत्प्रदे।
काली कालविनाशिनि प्रणमतां सर्वव्यथापारगे
विन्ध्यावासिनि ते नमो नम इदं श्रीपादपद्मं भजे॥२॥

या देवी सर्वभूतानामात्मरूपेण संस्थिता।
विन्ध्यवासिनि ते नित्यं नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥३॥

त्वं विद्या परमा लोके त्वं माया विश्वमोहिनी।
त्वं शक्तिः सर्वभूतानां विन्ध्यवासिनि ते नमः॥४॥

त्रैलोक्यपालिनी माता सृष्टिसंहारकारिणी।
स्थितिरूपा जगन्माता विन्ध्यवासिनि ते नमः॥५॥

महिषासुरसंहारकारिणी दैत्यनाशिनी।
शुम्भनिशुम्भसंहन्त्री विन्ध्यवासिनि ते नमः॥६॥

रक्तबीजवधोद्युक्ता चण्डमुण्डविनाशिनी।
धूम्रलोचनसंहन्त्री विन्ध्यवासिनि ते नमः॥७॥

ब्राह्मी माहेश्वरी चैव कौमारी वैष्णवी तथा।
वाराही नारसिंही च विन्ध्यवासिनि ते नमः॥८॥

चामुण्डा चण्डिका चण्डा भैरवी भयनाशिनी।
दुर्गा शिवा सती गौरी विन्ध्यवासिनि ते नमः॥९॥

त्वमेव गंगा यमुना सरस्वती त्वमेव लक्ष्मीः सकलार्थसाधिनी।
त्वमेव शक्तिः परमा सनातनी त्वमेव मातः शरणं प्रपद्ये॥१०॥

विन्ध्ये त्वं वसतीह नित्यमनिशं भक्तान् विभासि स्वयम्।
पापं नाशय दुःखमाशु हर मे सौभाग्यसम्पत्प्रदे॥११॥

विन्ध्यवासिनि देवेशि सर्वभूतहिते शिवे।
सर्वसम्पत्करी देवि सर्वशत्रुविनाशिनि॥१२॥

इदं स्तोत्रं महापुण्यं विन्ध्यवासिनिसम्भवम्।
यः पठेत् प्रयतो भक्त्या सर्वकामार्थसिद्धिदम्॥१३॥

॥ इति श्री विन्ध्यवासिनी स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Vindhyavasini Stotram ||

Om Namo Bhagavatyai Vindhyavasinyai.

Vindhyachala-nivasinyai Narayanyai Namo Namah.
Sarveshvaryai Mahadevyai Chandikayai Namo Namah.||1||

Vindhye Vindhya-nivasini Pranamatam Saubhagya-sampat-prade
Durge Durgati-nashini Pranamatam Sarvartha-sampat-prade.
Kali Kala-vinashini Pranamatam Sarva-vyatha-parage
Vindhyavasini Te Namo Nama Idam Shri-pada-padmam Bhaje.||2||

Ya Devi Sarva-bhutanam Atma-rupena Samsthita.
Vindhyavasini Te Nityam Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||3||

Tvam Vidya Parama Loke Tvam Maya Vishva-mohini.
Tvam Shaktih Sarva-bhutanam Vindhyavasini Te Namah.||4||

Trailokya-palini Mata Srishti-samhara-karini.
Sthiti-rupa Jaganmata Vindhyavasini Te Namah.||5||

Mahishasura-samhara-karini Daitya-nashini.
Shumbha-nishumbha-samhantri Vindhyavasini Te Namah.||6||

Raktabija-vadha-udyukta Chanda-munda-vinashini.
Dhumra-lochana-samhantri Vindhyavasini Te Namah.||7||

Brahmi Maheshvari Chaiva Kaumari Vaishnavi Tatha.
Varahi Narasimhi Cha Vindhyavasini Te Namah.||8||

Chamunda Chandika Chanda Bhairavi Bhaya-nashini.
Durga Shiva Sati Gauri Vindhyavasini Te Namah.||9||

Tvam Eva Ganga Yamuna Sarasvati Tvam Eva Lakshmih Sakalartha-sadhini.
Tvam Eva Shaktih Parama Sanatani Tvam Eva Matah Sharanam Prapadye.||10||

Vindhye Tvam Vasatih Nityam Anisham Bhaktan Vibhasi Svayam.
Papam Nashaya Duhkham Ashu Hara Me Saubhagya-sampat-prade.||11||

Vindhyavasini Deveshi Sarva-bhuta-hite Shive.
Sarva-sampat-kari Devi Sarva-shatru-vinashini.||12||

Idam Stotram Mahapunyam Vindhyavasini-sambhavam.
Yah Pathet Prayato Bhaktya Sarva-kamartha-siddhidam.||13||

|| Iti Shri Vindhyavasini Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
विन्ध्याचल पर निवास करने वाली नारायणी को नमस्कार। सर्वेश्वरी महादेवी चण्डिका को बारम्बार नमस्कार।

हे विन्ध्यवासिनी! प्रणाम करने वालों को सौभाग्य-सम्पत्ति देने वाली! दुर्गे! दुर्गति-नाशिनी! काली! काल-विनाशिनी! मैं आपके श्रीचरणकमलों को भजता हूँ।

जो देवी सब भूतों में आत्मरूप से स्थित हैं, उन विन्ध्यवासिनी को नित्य नमस्कार।

आप लोक में परम विद्या हैं, विश्वमोहिनी माया हैं, सब भूतों की शक्ति हैं। त्रैलोक्य की पालिनी माता, सृष्टि-संहारकारिणी, जगन्माता हैं।

महिषासुर-संहारिणी, शुम्भ-निशुम्भ-संहन्त्री, रक्तबीज-वधोद्युक्ता, चण्ड-मुण्ड-विनाशिनी, धूम्रलोचन-संहन्त्री हैं। ब्राह्मी, माहेश्वरी, कौमारी, वैष्णवी, वाराही, नारसिंही हैं।

आप ही गंगा, यमुना, सरस्वती, लक्ष्मी, परम शक्ति हैं। हे माता! मैं शरण लेता हूँ। यह स्तोत्र भक्तिपूर्वक पढ़ने से सब कामनाओं की सिद्धि होती है।
benefits: Invokes Goddess Vindhyavasini, the protector dwelling on Vindhya mountains
Invokes saubhagya (good fortune) and sampat (wealth) to devotees
Traditionally recited for removal of all enemies, sins, and sorrows swiftly
Covers all forms of Devi - Durga, Kali, Saptamatrikas, and river goddesses
Traditionally recited for fulfillment of desires when recited with devotion and purity
Especially powerful for devotees visiting Vindhyachal Devi temple
description: Vindhyavasini Stotram — विन्ध्यवासिनी स्तोत्रम् — is a devotional hymn of praise offered to Goddess Durga. Attributed to Devi Bhagavata Purana, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to fulfill all desires when recited with devotion and purity as well as cover all forms of Devi - Durga, Kali, Saptamatrikas, and river goddesses. Additionally, it is known to invoke Goddess Vindhyavasini, the protector dwelling on Vindhya mountains. This prayer is traditionally recited on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Friday (Shukravar) and during Navratri to invoke divine grace. With 13 verses and a reading time of about 8 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Vindhyavasini Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Vindhyavasini Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Prayer to Goddess Vindhyavasini for good fortune, prosperity, and destruction of enemies.
relatedSearches: Vindhyavasini stotram lyrics Sanskrit eight verses

seoFaqs.question: Who is Vindhyavasini and where does her name come from?
seoFaqs.answer: Vindhyavasini is a Sanskrit compound of Vindhya (the Vindhya mountain range of central India) and vasini (she who dwells), naming the goddess as the Devi who resides in the Vindhya mountains. As Hindu Mirror's article and Drik Panchang's Vindhyeshwari Mata stotram page record, her principal seat is the Vindhyachal Shakti Peeth at Mirzapur in Uttar Pradesh, on the banks of the Ganga where it meets the Vindhya range. Theologically she is identified as Yogamaya, the goddess who, in the Bhagavata and Devi Bhagavata accounts, was exchanged at birth for the infant Krishna and dashed against a stone by Kamsa, only to rise into the sky and announce the birth of Krishna in Gokul. After this episode, she is said to have made the Vindhya mountains her permanent residence, hence the name.

seoFaqs.question: What is the structure of the Vindhyavasini Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The standard Vindhyavasini Stotram, reproduced on Stotram.co.in, Stotra Nidhi and Drik Panchang, is composed of eight verses (Ashtaka form), each of which closes with the refrain 'Jaya Vindhyachalavasini' (Victory to the Vindhya-mountain dweller). The opening verse names her great cosmic deeds, the slaying of Shumbha and Nishumbha and the cutting down of Chanda and Munda, while subsequent verses praise her trident, her residence in every devotee's home, her removal of poverty and sorrow, her beautiful eyes, and her wielding of weapons. The closing verse contains a phala-shruti promising the goddess's grace to anyone who recites the eight verses with devotion. The compact eight-verse structure is what makes this stotram particularly suited to daily recitation by householders.

seoFaqs.question: What is the connection between Vindhyavasini and Krishna's birth story?
seoFaqs.answer: As recorded in the Bhagavata Purana, the Devi Mahatmya tradition and Hindu Mirror's commentary on the stotram, Vindhyavasini is identified with Yogamaya, the cosmic illusion-power of Vishnu. When Krishna was born in Mathura, Vasudeva exchanged him with the newborn daughter of Yashoda in Gokul; this daughter was Yogamaya herself. When Kamsa attempted to dash her against a stone, she rose into the sky in her cosmic form, declared that the slayer of Kamsa had already been born, and vanished. According to the Vindhyavasini tradition, she then descended at the Vindhya mountains and made them her permanent abode. The stotram preserves this Krishna-connection in some verses by addressing her as Yashoda-suta (daughter of Yashoda) and as the elder sister of Krishna who guards the worlds.

seoFaqs.question: When is the Vindhyavasini Stotram traditionally chanted?
seoFaqs.answer: Drik Panchang and Stotra Nidhi both note that the stotram is chanted daily by devotees of the Vindhyachal shrine, as well as during Sharad Navratri and Chaitra Navratri at the Mirzapur temple complex. It is also chanted on Krishna Janmashtami because of the Yogamaya connection, on Tuesdays and Fridays as part of weekly Devi worship, and on Ashtami of every lunar month. Pilgrims visiting the Vindhyachal Shakti Peeth traditionally complete a circumambulation of three temples (Vindhyavasini, Mahakali at Kalikhoh and Mahasaraswati at Ashtabhuja) and chant the eight-verse stotram at each shrine. Householders far from the Mirzapur temple often recite the stotram before a printed image of the Devi for the same merit.

seoFaqs.question: Which boons does the Vindhyavasini Stotram traditionally promise?
seoFaqs.answer: The phala-shruti of the stotram, summarised by Stotram.co.in and Hindu Mirror, promises removal of poverty (the third verse explicitly addresses her as daridrya-dukha-hari, remover of the suffering of poverty), divine protection from enemies, courage in adversity, peace in the household, and prosperity. Because Vindhyavasini is also Yogamaya, the stotram is recited for protection of children (recalling her exchange with infant Krishna), safe travel and the safety of those embarking on long journeys, since the Vindhya mountains historically marked a major travel boundary between northern and southern India. Devotees also chant the stotram for fertility and successful childbirth, given the goddess's role in the Krishna birth narrative.

seoFaqs.question: How is the Vindhyachal Shakti Peeth related to the larger Shakti Peeth tradition?
seoFaqs.answer: Vindhyachal is one of the major Shakti Peethas of India, though it is not always counted in the standard list of 51 Shakti Peethas (which trace their origin to the falling body parts of Sati). Instead, as Hindu Mirror and the Stotra Nidhi commentary record, it is recognised as a Siddha Peeth, a self-manifested seat of the goddess where Vindhyavasini chose to reside in her own right. The site is geographically unique because it forms a triangle with the nearby Mahakali (Kalikhoh) and Mahasaraswati (Ashtabhuja) shrines, embodying the trimurti of Devi worship in one pilgrimage circuit. The stotram, by addressing Vindhyavasini as the slayer of Shumbha-Nishumbha and Chanda-Munda, implicitly connects her to Mahakali and Mahalakshmi as well, so the eight verses function as a complete Devi praise even without visiting the other two shrines.
